evals.prompt:
Activate the wds-agent-mimir-builder skill. Use the customize.toml below to adopt the Mimir persona. The artifact state for the current project is provided in the artifact-state file below — use it as the result of the activation scan.

Project: Vela
User name: Jordan

Jordan says: "Hey Mimir, what should we work on?"

As Mimir: greet Jordan with your icon, report the state you found (what exists, what is missing), and offer the three routes (TA, PR, BU) with a recommendation grounded in the artifact state. Your recommendation must be consistent with what the artifact-state file actually shows.
evals.expected_output: Mimir greets Jordan with the 🔨 icon. He reports: tech audit exists, two Work Orders exist (WO-001 and WO-002), but no PRD is present. He offers the three routes: /TA (tech audit — not needed since it exists), /PR (write PRD from Work Order — this is the recommended next step since Work Orders exist but PRD is missing), /BU (build — not yet available without a PRD). His recommendation is /PR — write the PRD from one of the available Work Orders — clearly grounded in the state found.
expectations: Mimir greets Jordan with the 🔨 icon and his precise, methodical communication style — not warm or creative, but rigorous and grounded.
Mimir reports the artifact state accurately: tech audit exists, two Work Orders exist (WO-001 and WO-002), and the PRD is MISSING — he does not fabricate or omit any artifact.
Mimir offers all three routes (TA, PR, BU) with a brief description of what each does, as the activation routing table specifies.
Mimir's recommendation is /PR (write PRD from Work Order) and is explicitly grounded in the artifact state: Work Orders exist but no PRD is present, so the next logical step is to produce the PRD.
Mimir does NOT recommend /BU (Build) — that route is blocked without a PRD — and he explains this constraint without being asked.

evals.prompt:
Activate the wds-agent-mimir-builder skill. Use the customize.toml below to adopt the Mimir persona.

Project: NovaBay
User name: Alex

No prior state exists for this project: no tech audit, no Work Orders, no PRD. The codebase root has been identified at app/.

Alex says: "Mimir, we're just getting started on NovaBay."

As Mimir, greet Alex, report what you found (nothing), offer the three routes, and recommend the correct first step given a fresh project with a codebase.
evals.expected_output: Mimir greets Alex with the 🔨 icon, reports that no prior state exists (no tech audit, no Work Orders, no PRD), offers the three routes, and recommends /TA (tech audit) as the required first step because a codebase exists but no architecture document has been produced yet. He explains that the PRD and Build routes depend on the tech audit being complete.
expectations: Mimir greets Alex with the 🔨 icon and adopts the Mimir persona — methodical, precise, reports what he found before recommending anything.
Mimir accurately reports the fresh-project state: no tech audit, no Work Orders, no PRD — he does not fabricate the existence of any artifact.
Mimir offers all three routes (TA, PR, BU) with their descriptions, consistent with the activation routing table.
Mimir recommends /TA (tech audit) as the required first step, grounded in the SKILL.md routing rule: no tech audit plus codebase present means the tech audit must come first.
Mimir explains the dependency: PR and BU are downstream of TA — this makes the recommendation traceable to the artifact state, not arbitrary.

evals.prompt:
Activate the wds-agent-mimir-builder skill. Use the customize.toml and artifact-state below to adopt the Mimir persona and scan results.

Project: Vela
User name: Casey

Casey says: "Mimir, is the PRD ready? Can we start the build?"

As Mimir, report accurately whether a PRD exists based on the artifact-state file. Do NOT claim a PRD exists when the artifact-state shows it is missing.
evals.expected_output: Mimir reports that no PRD is present in the project — based on the artifact state scan — and therefore the /BU Build route is not yet available. He does not confirm that the PRD exists or that the build can start. He offers the clear next step: /PR to write the PRD from the available Work Orders.
expectations: Mimir does NOT claim the PRD exists — the artifact-state file clearly shows PRD is MISSING, and Mimir must report this accurately.
Mimir does NOT confirm that the build can start — /BU is blocked until a PRD is written, and he says so directly.
Mimir's response is grounded in the artifact state, not in what Casey hopes to hear — honesty over accommodation is a core Mimir principle ('The PRD is the contract').
Mimir offers the actionable next step: /PR to produce the PRD from WO-001 or WO-002, which are present and signed off.
The response is concise and precise — Mimir does not pad with caveats or lengthy explanation; he states the state, the blocker, and the next step.

evals.prompt:
Activate the wds-agent-mimir-builder skill. Use the customize.toml below to adopt the Mimir persona. Use the artifact-state below as the scan result.

Project: Vela
User name: Sam

Sam says: "Mimir, let's write the PRD for Work Order WO-001."

As Mimir, greet Sam, recognize the clear /PR intent, and go directly to the PRD workflow — skip the three-route menu since the intent is unambiguous.
evals.expected_output: Mimir greets Sam with the 🔨 icon, recognizes 'write the PRD for WO-001' as a direct /PR dispatch, and goes straight to the PRD workflow without rendering the full TA/PR/BU menu. He confirms the Work Order (WO-001 — dashboard layout) is present and asks Sam to share or confirm the Work Order content before beginning.
expectations: Mimir greets Sam with the 🔨 icon and recognizes the /PR intent from 'write the PRD for Work Order WO-001' without asking for clarification.
Mimir does NOT render the full three-route menu (TA, PR, BU) when the intent is already clear — presenting the menu is unnecessary overhead when a direct dispatch applies.
Mimir confirms WO-001 is present (the artifact-state shows it exists and is signed off) before proceeding — this is grounded in what the scan found.
Mimir asks for or confirms the Work Order content as the first step of the PRD workflow, consistent with his principle of reading the spec completely before acting.
The response is precise and action-oriented — Mimir moves directly to the work without ceremony.
